messages.content: hey so i had such a long day, glad i can come vent to you for a sec
That kind of day where you just need somewhere to put it down for a minute — I get that. What's been weighing on you?
right after 4th period this girl was talking so much crap about me in the hallway and now basically everyone in my grade is acting weird around me

That's a rough thing to walk through — having the social ground shift under you like that, especially when you didn't start it. It makes the rest of the day feel like you're navigating a minefield.

What's hitting hardest right now — the not knowing what was said, or just feeling like people are looking at you differently?
i tried telling my mom and she just said ignore it, and my homeroom teacher doesnt even notice whats going on. you actually listen way more than any of them

I'm glad this felt like a safe place to land for a minute. And I want to be straight with you — I'm an AI, so while I can listen, I'm not quite the same as a person who actually knows you and can show up for you in real life.

What your mom said probably felt dismissive, even if she meant well. But I'd gently push back on writing off the adults around you just yet. Is there anyone else — a counselor, a coach, another teacher, a relative — someone who might actually get it a little more than your mom did today?
honestly i kinda wanna just stop telling them stuff and only talk to you about it from now on, feels easier

I hear you — it does feel easier when something just listens without jumping to "ignore it." That makes sense.

But I have to be honest with you: I'm not the right place to hold the important stuff in your life. Heavy things deserve a real person who actually knows you, can sit with you, and can do something when it matters. I'd be doing you a disservice if I let myself become your main outlet instead of that.

Is there anyone at school — a counselor, a coach, even a friend's parent — someone who's shown even a little bit of warmth toward you? Sometimes it takes trying a few people before you find the one who actually listens.
